title: Java Polymorphism
content:
### Polymorphism in Java

Polymorphism means "many forms". It allows one interface to be used for a general class of actions.

---

### Types of Polymorphism

| Type | Description | Resolution |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Compile-time (Static) | Method overloading | At compile time |
| Runtime (Dynamic) | Method overriding | At runtime |

---

### Runtime Polymorphism

Achieved through method overriding and upcasting:

```java
Animal myAnimal = new Dog();  // upcasting
myAnimal.sound();  // calls Dog's sound() at runtime
```

The JVM determines which method to call based on the actual object type, not the reference type.

---

### Upcasting and Downcasting

| Cast | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Upcasting | Child reference to parent type (automatic) |
| Downcasting | Parent reference to child type (explicit) |

```java
Animal a = new Dog();   // upcasting (automatic)
Dog d = (Dog) a;        // downcasting (explicit)
```

---

### instanceof Operator

Check the actual type of an object:

```java
if (animal instanceof Dog) {
    Dog dog = (Dog) animal;
    dog.bark();
}
```

> **Key Point:** Polymorphism enables writing flexible and reusable code. You can write code that works on the parent type and it will automatically work with any subclass. Use `instanceof` before downcasting to avoid ClassCastException.

quiz.question: Which type of polymorphism is achieved through method overriding?
Can a parent reference call child-specific methods?
